revision.comment: see [[Template talk:R#Deprecated or not?]], not deprecated
revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:FNCONV}} This page describes procedures to convert the references from {{fnote}} defined in the body of the article to {{ldr}} where the references are defined in the reference list. Such a conversion may be necessary in order to move text with many references from an article that uses the list-defined format to another article, or may be used to format all references in an article so that they all conform to the same format. It requires installation of the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool. ==Converting from the inline to the list-defined format== * The [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool can automatically convert all references to the list-defined format. * Use a [[regular expression]] search-and-replace operation to replace uses of {{tag|ref|o}} tags with the {{tl|r}} template. To do that, search for: , and replace it with {{r\|"$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. ==Converting from the list-defined to the inline format== # Use a [[regular expression]] search and replace operation to replace uses of the {{tl|r}} template with {{tag|ref|o}} tags. To do that, search for: \{\{r\|([^}]+)\}\}, and replace it with {{tag|ref|s|params=name="$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. # Copy and remove (cut) the entire {{tl|reflist}} template with the list of references. # Install the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool and click the "Segregate refs for editing" button. # Remove all the empty {{tag|ref|o}} tags from the lower text box. # Paste the list of references into the lower text box (after its existing contents). # Save the page, which merges the citation templates from the lower text box into the upper text box and removes the unused footnotes in the process. {{Wikipedia referencing}}

{{Wikipedia how to|H:FNCONV}} This page describes procedures to convert the references from {{fnote}} defined in the body of the article to {{ldr}} where the references are defined in the reference list. Such a conversion may be necessary in order to move text with many references from an article that uses the list-defined format to another article, or may be used to format all references in an article so that they all conform to the same format. It requires installation of the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool. ==Converting from the inline to the list-defined format== * The [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool can automatically convert all references to the list-defined format. * Use a [[regular expression]] search-and-replace operation to replace uses of {{tag|ref|o}} tags with the {{tl|r}} template. To do that, search for: , and replace it with {{r\|"$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. ==Converting from the list-defined to the inline format== # Use a [[regular expression]] search and replace operation to replace uses of the {{tl|r}} template with {{tag|ref|o}} tags. To do that, search for: \{\{r\|([^}]+)\}\}, and replace it with {{tag|ref|s|params=name="$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. # Copy and remove (cut) the entire {{tl|reflist}} template with the list of references. # Install the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool and click the "Segregate refs for editing" button. # Remove all the empty {{tag|ref|o}} tags from the lower text box. # Paste the list of references into the lower text box (after its existing contents). # Save the page, which merges the citation templates from the lower text box into the upper text box and removes the unused footnotes in the process. {{Wikipedia referencing}}

{{Wikipedia how to|H:FNCONV}} This page describes procedures to convert the references from {{fnote}} defined in the body of the article to {{ldr}} where the references are defined in the reference list. Such a conversion may be necessary in order to move text with many references from an article that uses the list-defined format to another article, or may be used to format all references in an article so that they all conform to the same format. It requires installation of the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool. ==Converting from the inline to the list-defined format== * The [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool can automatically convert all references to the list-defined format. * Use a [[regular expression]] search-and-replace operation to replace uses of {{tag|ref|o}} tags with the {{tl|r}} template. To do that, search for: , and replace it with {{r\|"$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. ==Converting from the list-defined to the inline format== # Use a [[regular expression]] search and replace operation to replace uses of the {{tl|r}} template with {{tag|ref|o}} tags. To do that, search for: \{\{r\|([^}]+)\}\}, and replace it with {{tag|ref|s|params=name="$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. # Copy and remove (cut) the entire {{tl|reflist}} template with the list of references. # Install the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool and click the "Segregate refs for editing" button. # Remove all the empty {{tag|ref|o}} tags from the lower text box. # Paste the list of references into the lower text box (after its existing contents). # Save the page, which merges the citation templates from the lower text box into the upper text box and removes the unused footnotes in the process. {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:New]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:FNCONV}} This page describes procedures to convert the references from {{fnote}} defined in the body of the article to {{ldr}} where the references are defined in the reference list. Such a conversion may be necessary in order to move text with many references from an article that uses the list-defined format to another article, or may be used to format all references in an article so that they all conform to the same format. It requires installation of the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool. ==Converting from the inline to the list-defined format== * The [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool can automatically convert all references to the list-defined format. * Use a [[regular expression]] search-and-replace operation to replace uses of {{tag|ref|o}} tags with the {{tl|r}} template. To do that, search for: , and replace it with {{r\|"$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. ==Converting from the list-defined to the inline format== # Use a [[regular expression]] search and replace operation to replace uses of the {{tl|r}} template with {{tag|ref|o}} tags. To do that, search for: \{\{r\|([^}]+)\}\}, and replace it with {{tag|ref|s|params=name="$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. # Copy and remove (cut) the entire {{tl|reflist}} template with the list of references. # Install the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool and click the "Segregate refs for editing" button. # Remove all the empty {{tag|ref|o}} tags from the lower text box. # Paste the list of references into the lower text box (after its existing contents). # Save the page, which merges the citation templates from the lower text box into the upper text box and removes the unused footnotes in the process. {{Wikipedia referencing}}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:FNCONV}} This page describes procedures to convert the references from {{fnote}} defined in the body of the article to {{ldr}} where the references are defined in the reference list. Such a conversion may be necessary in order to move text with many references from an article that uses the list-defined format to another article, or may be used to format all references in an article so that they all conform to the same format. It requires installation of the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool. ==Converting from the inline to the list-defined format== * The [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool can automatically convert all references to the list-defined format. * Use a [[:en:Regular expression|Regular expression]] search-and-replace operation to replace uses of {{tag|ref|o}} tags with the {{tl|r}} template. To do that, search for: , and replace it with {{r\|"$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. ==Converting from the list-defined to the inline format== # Use a [[:en:Regular expression|Regular expression]] search and replace operation to replace uses of the {{tl|r}} template with {{tag|ref|o}} tags. To do that, search for: \{\{r\|([^}]+)\}\}, and replace it with {{tag|ref|s|params=name="$1"}}, while checking the "Treat search string as a regular expression" checkbox. # Copy and remove (cut) the entire {{tl|reflist}} template with the list of references. # Install the [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] tool and click the "Segregate refs for editing" button. # Remove all the empty {{tag|ref|o}} tags from the lower text box. # Paste the list of references into the lower text box (after its existing contents). # Save the page, which merges the citation templates from the lower text box into the upper text box and removes the unused footnotes in the process. {{Wikipedia referencing}}
